title: Split a Bill
summary: Divide a shared bill fairly among a group of people.
learnerPromise: You will be able to split a group bill evenly or by what each person ordered.
whyItMatters: Sharing costs with friends or roommates happens often, and a fair split keeps everyone comfortable and avoids awkwardness later.
realLifeUses: Dividing a dinner bill among three friends after a meal out
Splitting the cost of a shared gift for someone
Sharing grocery costs when cooking a group meal
Dividing rideshare or taxi fares on a group trip
result: You can split any group bill fairly using one of two methods.
youWillLearn: Split a total evenly by dividing by the number of people
Assign each person the cost of what they ordered individually
Add tax and tip fairly to each person's share
Handle one person who paid and needs to be paid back
miniChallenge: Imagine a $60 dinner bill for three people; split it evenly and then try splitting by what each person ordered.
steps: Ask the group if they want to split evenly or pay for their own items.
If splitting evenly, add the subtotal, tax, and tip, then divide by the number of people.
If splitting by order, go down the receipt and mark each item with the person's name.
Calculate each person's subtotal, then add an equal share of tax and tip.
Offer payment options like cash, payment app, or one person paying and others sending their share.
completionCriteria: You calculated two ways to split the same bill
You can explain when even splitting is fair versus splitting by order
commonProblems: One person ordered much more or less than others
Someone only has cash while others want to use an app
Forgetting to include tax and tip in each person's share
A shared appetizer or drink that needs to be split among everyone
tips: Decide the split method before ordering to avoid awkward conversations later
Apps like Venmo, Cash App, or Zelle make paying friends back quick
If splitting by order feels too complicated, even splitting is usually fine among close friends
